title: National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration
subtitle: The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.
caption:
National policies for orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration

The United Nations measures whether national migration policies meet International Organization for Migration criteria in a variety of policy domains, to ensure orderly, safe, regular, and responsible migration.

The 2021 world map displays national migration policy performance using a five-category color scale ranging from red to dark blue, alongside a hatched pattern for missing data. The lightest blue shade, indicating that a country "Meets criteria," covers the largest geographic area, including the United States, Canada, Australia, Russia, and the majority of nations across Europe and South America. A darker blue band, representing countries that "Fully meets criteria," appears in isolated locations such as Peru, South Africa, and Iran. The orange category, labeled "Partially meets criteria," is prominent across much of Africa, including Nigeria and Ethiopia, as well as in China and India. The red spectrum denotes areas requiring further progress; a deep red shade is visible in Myanmar, while lighter red tones appear in countries like Libya and Yemen. Significant portions of the map feature diagonal hatching to indicate "No data," which includes Greenland, Western Sahara, and several nations in Central Africa and the Middle East. The distribution shows a concentration of the highest performance bands in the Americas and Oceania, while the middle and lower bands are distributed across Asia and Africa, interspersed with regions where data is unavailable.
